data.title: Year of the Horse is Bullish for Stocks—Nvidia's CEO Agrees
data.description:
Wall Street loves a good narrative, but it turns out the best market predictor might not be a Fed chair—it might be a stable. 

As Tuesday marks the beginning of the year of the Horse, investors are leaning into a quirky but historically consistent trend: the Chinese Zodiac's four-legged alpha.

  • NVDA stock is moving. See the chart and price action here. 

The Four-Legged Factor

Data suggests that the stock market has a distinct preference for animals that walk on all fours, as Ryan Detrick, chief market strategist at Carson Group, highlighted on X. 

According to historical S&P 500 performance data, the Horse, Goat, Ox, Pig and Dog represent the strongest performing years in the 12-year cycle. 

Conversely, two-legged signs—the Rooster and the Monkey—have historically sat at the bottom of the returns pile.

Historically, Horse years have delivered double-digit gains, fueled by the galloping energy the sign represents.

There is a humorous irony in the fact that the most bullish years come from animals with four legs, while years represented by animals with two legs or no legs tend to struggle. 

Nvidia Corp. (NASDAQ:NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang recently noted that the Horse is synonymous with speed, strength and forward momentum. 

“This year is the year of the Horse. So, it's going to be a very good year," Huang said. 

Animal Spirits

While serious analysts point to interest rates and earnings, the Zodiac effect taps into a psychological phenomenon. 

When a tech titan like Nvidia's Huang aligns a company's outlook with cultural symbols of prosperity, it bolsters sentiment, and believing the year of the Horse will be profitable can become a self-fulfilling prophecy in a market ruled by animal spirits. 

Photo: Shutterstock

data.title: This Albemarle Analyst Turns Bullish On Improving Structural Fundamentals
data.description:
The Albemarle Analyst: Analyst Rock Hoffman upgraded the rating from Neutral to Buy and raised the price target from $167 to $190.

The Albemarle Thesis: While the company's share price had earlier risen partly due to the ongoing rise in lithium spot prices, "recent developments suggest broader support," Hoffman said in the upgrade note.

Check out other analyst stock ratings.

Albemarle's structural fundamentals are improving, he stated.

The company reported its fourth-quarter results ahead of estimates, "supported by strong Energy Storage volumes," the analyst mentioned.

While delivering annualized cost savings of $450 million, Albemarle guided to a further $100-$150 million of "incremental cost and productivity improvements," supporting full-year EBITDA guidance of $2.4-$2.6 billion, much higher than consensus of $1.7 billion, he further wrote.

ALB Price Action: Shares of Albemarle had risen by 1.75% to $169.26 at the time of publication on Tuesday.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: OLB Group Stock Skyrockets On PayPal Partnership
data.description:
• OLB Group shares are climbing with conviction. Why is OLB stock surging?

Partnership Brings PayPal, Venmo, Pay Later

The deal authorizes OLB to integrate and promote PayPal's checkout and wallet solutions across its SecurePay gateway and other commerce platforms.

Through the partnership, OLB merchants will gain access to PayPal Checkout, Venmo, PayPal Pay Later and PayPal Credit, along with PayPal's fraud protection and cross-border payment tools.

Per OLB Group, the integration is designed to simplify merchant onboarding, consolidate settlement and reporting and expand access to buyers in more than 200 markets.

OLB Targets Higher Payment Volumes, New Merchants

The rollout is scheduled to begin in phases starting in the first quarter of 2026, with initial availability focused on existing SecurePay merchants.

Short-Term Momentum Builds

OLB stock is currently trading 12.5% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA), indicating short-term strength, while it is 20.8% below its 100-day SMA, suggesting some longer-term challenges.

Over the past 12 months, shares have decreased by 34.65%, and they are currently positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs.

The RSI is not available, but the MACD status remains neutral, indicating that the stock is not showing strong bullish or bearish momentum at this time. The combination of neutral indicators suggests that traders should remain cautious as the stock navigates its current price levels.

  • Key Resistance: $1.50
  • Key Support: 75 cents

The overall market has been experiencing fluctuations, with various sectors reacting differently to economic indicators. While some sectors are under pressure due to concerns about inflation and interest rates, fintech stocks like OLB have benefited from a shift in consumer behavior towards digital solutions.

OLB Group’s Fintech Services

The OLB Group is a diversified fintech e-commerce merchant services provider and Bitcoin crypto mining enterprise. It focuses on a suite of products in the merchant services marketplace and seeks to provide integrated business solutions to merchants throughout the United States.

The company’s offerings include a cloud-based business management platform that provides turnkey solutions for merchants to enable them to build and manage their retail businesses, whether online or at a brick-and-mortar location.

OLB Shares Skyrocket Tuesday

OLB Price Action: OLB Group shares were up 137.32% at 99 cents at the time of publication on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock
